reviews.review_msg: You don't need the textbook or to read the slides, just use the tutorial slides and the practice midterm given by the TA's to study. Both TAs are fantastic. His exams were so easy. Beware of high curve, almost everyone i know got curved down. Shafik as a prof is good but overhyped, his lectures are not that useful, tends to cover 4 slides a lect.

Shafik was kind and helpful. His exam was fair, he tested you on materials that were taught in lecture. His TA was also kind and helpful. I personally like this class because the course materials are fun. You don't only memorize but analyze too. Overall, I suggest you to take bus 393 with Shafik.
Shafik is a gem. Best professor I've ever had. Exams are a cakewalk. Don't really need the textbook. TAs provide plenty of review material so as long as you drill that there will be 0 issues.

Everyone I know has hyped this man up, but after taking a class with him, I was really disappointed. He likes to go on tangents during lectures and sometimes they're relevant, usually not. Slides and readings are disorganized, extremely unclear grading, oh and don't bother participating because he gives everyone a good participation grade anyway.

HE IS THE BEST PROF IN BEEDIE NGL
he will make everything easy, and he will give you many examples to make the concepts not that tough. 
Personally think he is the only prof worth me to pay such international tuition
Shafik is great! Pretty easy class, just study lecture/tutorial slides, do any practice exams provided and you'll do fine.

Brilliant! Brilliant! Brilliant! He is the only prof that I don't want him to end the lecture early!

The course has a tight curve so the final exam determines everything. Concepts are mostly discussed during class and he *will* test you on the final (e.g. Meiorin test, substratum doctrine). Know the presentation cases as well, they are testable.

Shafik's lectures are super interesting and he gives lots of good examples that stick with you for the exams (so you should attend lecture). The class is fairly easy with a light workload for a 300-level class but the tradeoff is high a average so you have to score high to get above the curve. Shafik was awesome and hope I can have him again.

Had him in BUS 303 B4, and took him for law too. He's a great prof, and I study for 3 hrs the morning of the exam and it's a breeze. Avgs are high tho, making it competitive to do well. Just get perfect on the tutorial, highest mark on the prez, and hopefully get above curve on the exam to secure the bag. Course is good w/ other tough quant classes

Taken 2 classes with him 393 & 303. Hands down, great prof. Super consistent, always prepared, & makes the material make sense. He doesn't just read off slides. Used tons of real-life examples. Also really funny, keeps the class from ever getting boring. But heads up — don't use your phone in his class.

Best course, best teacher at Beedie School. The class is One of the most exciting classes I have taken with this teacher. He makes the subject exciting and gets all of us involved in participating and critically analyzing cases and subjects. He does it in a way that is very inviting and fun. I love his classes. Class is always full.

He is a lawyer who teaches this course and so very well. I hate early morning classes but his I looked forward to. He make a subject that is so dry very “sexy” and exciting with real world examples from his experience. I love that he gives context and then hypotheticals that we can relate to. I am really thinking of going into law.

By far the best teacher I have come across. It is such a pleasure to be in his class. He makes the environment extremely fun to learn in, and he truly cares about seeing everyone succeed! I would 100% recommend taking his class.

Took BUS 303 with Shafik this semester. Grading criteria is unclear. He is arrogant and egotistical and does not hide it one bit. He always drifts off to random subjects, which is useless for students. So many examples taught in class were no use for studying. People recommend Shafik, but take it with anyone else, it's not a hard subject.

Excellent! Best in my three years at BEEDIE. Knows the material well. Using his book on the subject which has some great study materials. Love his Friday classes. He is very entertaining when teaching. Love his classes. He does not suffer lazy students and those using cellphones in his class.  Taking employment law with him  now

I have taken two courses with Shafik so far (BUS 233 and BUS393) both of which have been incredible. He is such an amazing prof with his humour, real life examples to connect to lectures, and stories from his career being a lawyer. I hope to take couple more courses with him for sure. There is a reason his classes fill up so fast and get full!
There are easier professors for this class.. for the sake of yourself, avoid him.

Shafik made lectures enjoyable. His exams are also very fair. The group project could use more structure/instructions. Will make plenty of jokes which I loved (not for the easily offended). Teaches using examples, which is how you will be evaluated. Will take more courses with him even though I don't need to.
Shafik is a great prof! He is a funny guy and teaches well. Exams are fair as well, just wish you're not on his bad books and don't get bullied every lecture.

There really isn't anything to study in this course. Getting an B+/A- is incredibly easy, but doing any better than that seems impossible because of the curve. He has a dry sense of humour, which is at times funny, and other times rude.
Engaging lectures and useful guidance from the prof. For study prep, ChatGPT and unstuckstudy ai were super helpful alongside the free course materials. Overall, a rewarding experience.

He is a good proffesor and there is absolutely no doubt about this. He knows his stuff very well, uses different examples from the class to explain the stuff. The average for the course is so high.  If I would get a chance to do this course again, I wont do with him at all so that I can get a better grade! If you want an A, dont take with him.

Loved the content of the class, and the mark I got was not bad. the issue here is the prof. extremely rude, ill mannered, and finds joy out of embarrassing his students. he should have some shame. dont ever book a phone call w him- he will use that time to degrade you

Super engaging and funny lectures. For the law assignment, he gives you a sample you can follow. For the ethics exam, he guides you on how to approach the questions that will be on the final. He also posts slides and lecture recordings. However, think before asking him for help. He doesn't like it if you email him late at night.

Good lectures and funny prof, but be warned; he is ideologically possessed, very old school, rude when you talk to him or approach for questions. Take James Pflanz instead, at least he doesn't yell at me over the phone when i request office hours.
This guy is the goat. He made fun of the kid beside me all year and it made my day
Shafik = Lebron
He is super amazing. He is the best prof I have had at SFU sp far, and I would love to take his class in the future.

Amazing professor, hands down one of the best professors at SFU. He is caring and engaging, I actually learnt stuff in his class as opposed to other profs who might have a very qualified degree but know nothing in their discipline.

Truly the best prof at SFU. Shafik actually cares about your wellbeing and ensures that his class is fair. The topics were so interesting and Shafik's humour made 3 hours feel like nothing. I actually was sad when class was over. Best class I've ever taken.

Best SFU prof ever ! would recommend to anyone who wants to learn and enjoy school! He is hilarious, and great at teaching. You can tell he cares, which is rare to find in a prof. I never write reviews but he is honestly a rare prof, as half the profs at SFU don't care.

I found the prof very engaging and extremely knowledgeable. I am not sure about the student in the review below but I found him very respectful and fair and very approachable. In fact he was my best prof last year. I thought the marking was very fair too. I am actually taking his other course this semester and so far very consistent

Shafik is hands down the best professor at SFU Beedie! He is funny, caring, and engaging. Shafik conveys lecture material clearly and supports the concepts with real life examples. Lectures are intriguing and he always encourages participation. Exams are based on concepts and examples covered in class. Take BUS393 with Shafik, you won't regret it!

Shafik has been one of the best professors I've had at SFU. I highly recommended attending his lectures as the concepts are explained in depth with many examples. He often engages the class to participate and give their opinions on the subject matter. The exams were fair and tested on material covered throughout the slides.

Shafik is the best professor for the Bus393 Commercial Law course. He's been very supportive throughout the semester and the way he keeps everyone involved in the class is excellent and laudable. I'd highly suggest everyone to take this course with him.
The best professor I've had at SFU. He's a great lecturer and explains the material through real life examples which makes it so much easier to learn. I highly recommend attending his lectures!

Easily the best prof I've ever had. Despite long lectures, it was super easy to engage and understand topics with him using unique stories and examples. He also had practical real-world experiences and stories which was super cool to hear. Studying for the midterm was relatively easy because of this although it is somewhat tricky but rewarding.
Best professor I've had so far. Attend lectures and you'll understand why. 10/10 would recommend.
Amazing prof. he is one of the best profs at SFU.

I would highly recommend Shafik. He has clear grading criteria, encourages participation, and makes lectures interesting. His midterm exam is extremely fair, and if you miss a lecture he provides lecture recordings on canvas. The papers are pretty easy and there isn't much work you have to do outside of class.

As a non-business major this has been my favourite class I have ever taken in my 3 years at SFU. Shafik is a rare prof. who is a combination of humour, genuinely has care for his students, and thoroughly teaches content with clarity. He is the most fair prof. I've had and if you ever get a chance to take a class with him, you'll forever cherish it.

The professor i came across as an international student in canada. He cares for students and has a good sense of humour. I took this course bcoz i have intrest in law and the lectures were so intresting. Highly recommended without a doubt he is the best and knowledgeable.

It felt really good to know how much he cares about his students. One of the best Professors I've had at SFU! he's also very interesting and tries his best to answer all your questions. There aren't a lot of thing to do (3- midterm, assignment, final) all worth 30% so you'll need to do well on all to get a good mark but its most definitely doable

One of the best profs I've ever had. His grading scheme and exams are fair and he is good at getting students to understand the material. He's hilarious and it makes going to lecture way more enjoyable. He is very helpful and he really cares about his students and wants to them succeed. Very cool dude and very inspiring.
Great professor, always open to help.
Best professor in Beedie.
Shafik is the GOAT

I took Bus 303 this semester with professor bhalloo and honestly all of the reviews on here are very accurate. I am not a morning person but still found myself attending his Friday morning lectures. He is super funny, approachable and uses real life scenarios to make the course engaging. 1 midterm, 1 final and a group project!

I thought employment law course for business in MBA/MOT program would be dry but somewhat relevant. It turned out to be the most interesting of my courses and very relevant and informative. Shafiq is a great prof. He is a practicing lawyer and, like most reviews here say, he brings his legal experience in the classroom and makes it exciting for all

One of the best profs i met ! Very genuine, really helpful and his classes are very interesting and interactive! He mostly gives real life examples which would really really help any student to understand the concepts better! Really fair with marking!

Shafik was a great prof that explained concepts clearly and did a really good job enforcing your learning. He covered topics in a fashion that keeps people entertained and interested. I really enjoyed his class!! There was no homework other than 1 group project (+ 1 midterm and 1 final), which means the workload is light (and they aren't hard too).

I like his style of teaching business ethics. I took both bus law and bus ethics with him and I. Both classes he was consistent. Very clear and easy to understand. I think the final was a bit difficult but he marked it very fairly . He genuinely wants you to succeed in class and is always available for help. My favourite teacher at Beedie
Funny guy and genuinely a good prof. Definitely recommend.
Shafik Bhalloo is a very caring prof and loves what he teaches. He provides loads of his own time if you choose to contact him. being available during most week days as well as some weekends.
Very good teacher! Would take any class with him again for sure. I had him for bus 233 and he was extremely understanding and good!

I was a little scared of taking this course but I had Shafiq for Bus Law An day loved it. What made this course excellently was his style of teaching and also his examples and how he interacted with us in class. Ed is re spectrum Andy very fair but also kind

I absolutely loved his Crim 1116 ,. It is clear he loves teaching and cares about his students and wants them to succeed. Having access to the lectures online is so helpful, and I really appreciate his exams; they are precisely what he teaches. You can get a good grade if you study his slides, listen to his lectures and attend checkin classes.

Shafik Bhalla was one of the best profs I had. His lectures are worth attending, and they are not boring at all. His style of teaching is very good and the main thing. When you write him an email about anything and if you mention your cell number, this guy CALLS YOU back within 2 hours. His exams are not that difficult tbh.

Shafik is excellent at explaining concepts in a clear and concise manner, as well as keeping lectures engaging. He is very clear when it comes to his expectations, so as long as you put an effort in you will end up with a good grade. He is dedicated and a good example of what students wish all profs could be like.

Shafik is one of my all-time favourite profs at SFU. He is super understanding, and accommodates his teaching style for the benefit of his students, especially in online school. He is accessible outside of class time and will make sure topics are completely understood. Lectures are fun, participation helps, and you won't even want to skip class!

Shafik is an amazing prof. He is always accessible outside classroom and he gives 120% respect to everyone. Shafik will also try his best to answer every single question you ask and he will never rush for materials. My only comment is: Please go for him with this course because you will take more than you expected.

Shafik has easily become one of my top favourite instructors on my 2 years in SFU. Hes kind , hilarious and truly I MEAN TRULY cares for his students. Hes passionate about his work and it shows in class. by the writing of this I have not received my grade, so this is my unbiased opinion . If youre even slightly interested in bus law I recomd it
Great adaptation for the online style. Great care for the students. Great life stories.

Like Shafiq as my teacher very much. His lectures are excellent. Never miss them. He gives lots of examples and he relates the material to stuff we can understand. We finished midterm exam and unlike some teachers he covers only stuff we have covered in class in exam and marks were very fair. He will call you back on weekends if you have questions
the guy starts talking shop the moment you ask a question

Shafik is by far one of my favourite teachers. He is always accessible and wants you to succeed in every way possible. He focuses on teaching until the whole class understands. This means he will never rush his lectures and will dedicate 3 to sometimes 4 classes for one chapter. Make sure to take Shafik if you ever get the opportunity.

I am there at 8:30 a.m. for his class, and on Fridays yeah! I love this prof. His classes are great. He involves you in lectures when he gives examples. Makes things crystal clear. Need more like him. Good luck getting in his class. I waited to get in his class now for 2 semesters and finally
Amazing prof. You'll actually enjoy going to class. I dare you to sit in the front;) haha. Just study examples and terms, you'll do fine. :)

Shafiq clearly loves law by the passion he shows in his lecture. Attending his lectures is like going to theatre and you get a performance. It is like being entertained while you are learning and it is painless:) he is very good. One of my favourite teachers 4 sure.
Good teacher but lectures are a bit on the longer side sometimes. He could give a longer break in the class. Examples are very good but some examples a bit too long
Best prof I have had at Beedie, hands down. Waiting for the fall semester to have his other course in ethics. Will also take his employment Law course when he teaches.

Taking business ethics and also business law with him at SFU. His classes are always full, even the early morning one on Tuesday at 8:30 . He comes very prepared to perform. He is very good and has a way of making material interesting and enjoyable with real examples. He is always available to help, even okay taking calls at his office.

BEST PROF EVER! Took 393 with him never really studied just went to lecture and took notes, he gives really good examples so you don't really need to study hard for the midterm or final! But all his classes have really big waitlists which sucks! Really wanted to take 303 with him but the waitlist was all filled up. Wish he taught more classes
One of the best professor I have had at SFU. Great entertaining lectures. Read the book to get the harder questions on the exam.

Shafiq is an ecellent prof. Very caring and smart. I took his business ethics class and found him very good at explaning difficult concepts with examples. Fair marker. He loves people participating and very good at getting people to participate. Very respectful. I am taking bus 393 with him in the fall. Just got in his class. Was on waitlist.

Amazing lectures, easy to understand, always prepared and very engaging. I will take him again in ethics course business 303. I wish he would soon teach employment Law course. My friend took his employment course and said he was excellent.
Best teacher I have had at SFU by a mile and a half. I wish I had taken him in Bus 303 because his bus 393 class rocked. He is really good.

I took CRIM 1116 at Langara with Shafik and he was my favourite teacher because he was hilarious and taught the material effectively. The two downsides are he isn't approachable when you don't get something ,which effected my final exam mark and would've gotten it right had he explained it, and his exams are short but he tries to trick you in them.

Best professor at SFU by a mile. Shafik is engaging and makes the material so interesting to the point where it excites you to go to lectures and learn. Would highly recommend taking any class with the GOAT.

Amazing prof, never missed lecture! Love the way he teaches, everything just sticks with you when you are studying for exams. He has a strict no cell phone rule but overall a really funny and inspiring prof! If your taking 393, take it with him he's the best!

Loved Shafik, he is so great! He is very interesting to listen to since he has lots of passion for law and brings up hilarious examples in class to relate to the concepts he is teaching. Very easy to sit through the lectures because they are super entertaining, don't skip them tho he tests on what he covers in class. Never opened the book

Super duper guy. His lectures are really really good. He tells stories to explain difficult concepts Andy uses examples you can relate to in your own life which makes it understanle. He loves participation and will remember your name if you participate and use you in his examples. I am surprised he remembered my name in a class of 90 students.

I had prof Shafiq for bus 303 and enjoyed it so much that I have him for BUS393. He make law and ethics very easy to understand and he has really good example to break things down in bite sizes to make you understand. His lectures are really good. I am there at 8:30 in the morning. Not many classes in the last 3 years I have woken up for at 8:30

Shafik is one of the most committed professors I have had, he makes sure he is available for his students whenever they need it. He spoke to me on the phone for 40 minutes on a Sunday while he was at his law firm to help me feel more prepared by answering questions I had about our midterm. He truly cares, and is a prof cap is lucky to have.

Completed midterm exam last night in bus 393- business law with prof. Shafiq. His lectures are amazing. Really! You learn way more from him than the text book. He uses real world examples to make things easier to understand and he does it in a way you understand clearly. The midterm exam was very fair and No surprises if you attended lectures.

Amazing lectures. He is excellent and easy to listen to and super smart. Knows his stuff. Had him in commercial law and then in business ethics course last summer. Would take any of his classes as he is my favourite prof at SFU Beedie. Very approachable and always available to you. Treats you like a human being.

Of the teachers I have had, he is no doubt one of the best. He is a lawyer who knows how to speak very well and explain things with examples so that you do not get lost in class. He always asks you if you understand and rarely when you do not he will give more examples and makes sure he does not leave you behind. I wish more were like him. ����

He is an excellent teacher when it comes to explaining legal concepts with examples you can relate to. He is very available to students. He makes lectures so much fun. He called me back on thebwerkend when I needed help. Waitlists for his class are long.

Shafiks a nice guy and cares about his class however his refusal to use Capilano U's Moodle class portal makes for serious confusion about due dates and where to find assignments. Digging through countless emails about his kids' Saturday activities to find assignments is driving our class bananas. Takes far too long going through notes in lecture.

I had prof Bhalloo in the summer for both bus 303 and 393 in the summer session. I thought he was excellent and consistently great lectures in both classes. I have not seen him teach bus 483 recently but would love to take it when he teaches. He is very good. If you can get in his class good luck as always waitlists.

I like Shafiks lecture, never skip one class and understand the basic concepts of bus 303. The grade is straight forward, one midterm and one final assignment, same format, very similar to the example midterm. Practice and ask him. Be free to provide own experience to him at last question. But the average is high, got 82%, and maybe around median

Shafiks the best teacher I have had at sfu. I never miss his lectures ever ever. I wish the class was not at 8:30 in the morning but I have always made it as he makes the course so very interesting and fun. You learn so much and participate . I think his midterm was very fair. I am in both his bus303 ethics class also and he is consistently good.

Shafik is a great, caring guy. Engaging lectures. The class has gotten harder and isn't as easy to do well in. You're expected to answer beyond what questions are actually asking you to do. Tough to get feedback/explanations from TAs as their answers are akin to "ehh sorry, tough luck".

Agree with the last comment. Shafik is amazing prof. You cannot miss his great lectures in the bus 393 commercial Law class. He involves students in examples in class. He makes it easier for you to understand material and I found his tests fair both midterm and final. He is also very funny and smart. I am in his bus 303- ethics class this summer

Shafik is an awesome professor. He's an amazing lecturer and funny, I really like how he used real world examples. I actually wanted to come to lecture LOOL. Midterm average was around 75%. Textbook is useless. Short answer on the midterm and final is marked harshly. Use of the right terminology is crucial to the short answer section.

I have Shafik this semester in BUS393. He is an excellent prof. Do not miss his lectures. He is very good at explaining things. He is a great story teller with legal cases he has worked on to explain concepts to us. Learning with him is very fun. I really like him. If you have a choice go with him and you will see.

Shafik is a very engaging prof. who keeps lectures interesting by telling stories related to course material. Course content is quite easy, work load is light, and you can get by just using lecture notes. Attendance is not mandatory but highly recommended as his lectures really help you to understand the material. Great prof. highly recommend.

Phenomenal! I took him in buss393 business law and bus 303 business eithics and then bus483. Consistently excellent prof. Called him at his law office for help and he spent 15 mnts on the phone explaining stuff to me. Super knowledgeable about what he teaches. Would give him 2 thumbs up. If you have a choice go with him and hell not dissapoint you

Shafik is awesome! He cares so much about his students and is super accessible. Half the course is based on law, which I think he did a better job of teaching - made it super interesting by bringing in real world examples. The ethics part was bearable. Fair exams. Would love to take a class with him again!

Shafik is the best prof I've ever had. Sometimes he rambled on a bit long about some subjects, but overall was a great lecturer, very knowledgable with funny stories. If you show up to class you do not need to read the textbooks, he provides detailed notes. Some of the MC on Midterm were identical to Qs on the practice exam he gives.

he is my best prof in business. I took both bus393 and 303 with him. His lectures are great and he is clear and very fair. I am waitng to take bus483 wih him when he teaches again. Obviously I would take him again.
Awesome teacher. I would take him again. Very knowledgeable about his stuff and very interesting. Learned so much and enjoyed every class

as a practicing lawyer, he brings a lot of knowledge and passion to each class with relevant and current examples. great discussions and participation was encouraged. he was always available even when he was working from the law firm and sometimes even on weekends. First semester this course was offered, course load was light - highly recommend

Exam is tomorrow. He was available to take my question on Sunday and call me back. Thisbis service. I find lectures so unteresting. He is definitely very clear and makes materials very interesting. I never skip his classes. He is also very fair based on midterm. He wants you to do very well and will help you if you want to work hard. Great teacher.
Very very good. Professor. Top 1% of teachers in the department. I learned sooo much in his course. Yes, law was exciting. Never thought I would say it. I never missed a single lecture.

He was a really great and straight forward professor. He clearly outlined what would be on midterm and final. He gave a practice midterm and some of the same questions were on his actual midterm! However, he talks way too much and the class became such a drag at times. I never showed up to lecture, because he did send out slides!

I took Shafiks bus233 class in Business Law and ethics. I Really enjoyed his class. He made the subject very interesting. I like his style. Many excellent examples and very clear. I have decided to take a minor in business because of this class. I think he is very caring and really excellent prof.

I agree he is really good. I find him very easy and clear to understand. Many examples he gives. Usually examples you can relate and understand. Very helpful also and he honestly wants you to do well and is always available to assist. He is my favorite teacher in the Business school.

I have him for commercial law bus 393this semester and he is a great prof. You learn so much from him and he is definitely passionate about his teaching. It is good that he is a lawyer and gives great examples. I think he I sent very clear and I love his lectures. He involves you in examples and I am looking to take bus 483 with him

His lectures are interesting and based on good examples and experience. But his slides are very busy and assignments and tests require a LOT of attention to detail. You need to review a reasonable amount and communicate with TA about your group project early and often; high marks are achievable with this in mind

Best teacher at the Business school in my 4.5 years. I am going to do MBA and I wish we had teachers like him there. He knows his stuff so well and his classes are amazing. I took him for commercial law class, businEST ethics and now Employment Law. He is so interesting and passionate about the stuff he teaches. Big waitlists for his classes.

If you take commercial law or ethics course, hands down you have to do it with SHafik. He is da best. His lectures and style of teaching is great. He talks to you in lectures like you are involved in the class and his examples are crystal clear.
Took bus 303 class. Excellent teacher. Could not get in his law class 393. there was long waitlist and I could not get in. Will try again in the fall. He is my choice.

I like shafik because his lectures are very clear and he knows what he is talking about. Gives real world examples to make his point. He makes boring stuff exciting. He is honestly interested interested in making students comfortable and encourages us to participate in class. In the 3 years at school, I have to say he is the best prof I have had.

Took his commercial law bus 393.  Hewas just great. He made a difficult subject so easy to understand. He used examples students can relate to which made it really good. He is the best teacher I have taken in Beedie. Tried getting into his bus303 but it had a long waitlist and I hope to get in during summer. Taking his bus 483 employment law also

Taking both his courses this semester: business 393 commercial law and business 303 ethics. He is the best prof to take in both courses but it is difficult to get into his class. I got in last minute when the department opened the course and allowed some of the people on his huge waitlist to register. You have to take him.

He makes everything very simple and straightforward. 1 midterm, 1 group assignment, 1 final with SAME FORMAT! He explains the theories over and over + also breaks down structure of assignments with crystal clarity. If you didn't get a high mark in this class, then something's wrong with you not him. I couldn't have asked for a better prof for 303.

Very smart teacher. Was very clear in lectures and gave lots of real world examples to explain things. He was very fair to students and unlike some of my teachers, very respectful and treated us like we mattered. He actually remembers our names in the  large classes when we participate. Awesome teacher. Taking 303 with him next semester.
He explains the concepts very clearly and gives many examples to make sure students understand. The tests are a bit long, so you need to be quick. One of my best prof every :)

Heard that ethics is confusing and hard to get good grades before taking. However, Shafik does his best to make the class so straightforward and easy to follow. No need to read the book as his lecture notes included everything. It is good to attend classes to clarify confusions and listen to his examples. One of my best prof at SFU :)

303 is pretty straightforward. Teaching style is relaxed and friendly. Is always super open to students reaching out to him for questions. Pretty subjective class. 80% average on MT and 80% on assignment. Hard to get a high mark due to high curve.

Great professor. Very intriguing lectures and teaches the material well so that you understand it. Stand up guy! Marking criteria is clear and marks wise he is very generous but beware of the curve since it is so high. All in all would take any course with him again.

I laughed, I cried, I laughed again.  That has nothing to do with the class, I'm just bipolar. Shafik is terrific, his skill at making a point clear through his stories is awesome. He made sure to get to know almost everyone and we all felt more involved because of it.

Even when it's a crappy day, I never want to miss his lecture. He connects theories with his real life experiences, and you just never want to miss even one of his classes. I made a mistake not taking 303 with him. He cares a lot about his students, and is always accessible for help! Best prof ever.

Clear expectations and always accessible if you need clarification on class materials.  Quite a bit of reading but not impossible to manage.  The topics discussed are highly useful in the work environment.  No surprises on the exam but do require you to really understand the materials.  Lectures are funny with lot of stories, so you won't get bored

Totally awesome prof. I find his lectures very informative and so much practical info. It helps being able to lecture like he does. He is an excellent story teller and tons of examples to make you understand materials. Definitely most interesting class I have had.
He's hilarious. By far the best professor I have had at SFU. His lectures are mandatory, but they are more like a show than a lecture as he tells you to sit back and watch.

He is one of my favourite professors at SFU. He put effort in every lecture that he taught. His lectures were interesting and fun to listen to. He made good use of his real life experience. I do recommend taking this class with him cause nobody else can do it better.

Excellent teacher. Very helpful when you ask for help and will give you second chance if you screw up at midterm.  Very fair marker . Lectures are super good. Have to be lazy in this class to do poorly.

I am taking 303 and 393 with him this semester and I like his style so very much. he is very interesting and his lecture are excellent. do not take a cell phone to his class or you will get in trouble. I learned so much with him and he makes the class fun.

I too 393 and 303 with him and found him equally excellent in both classes. He is very clear and like all here his examples are very good. If you did not do well it is because you are lazy or not smart. He goes out of his way to help you. Excellent teach for sure.
